_metadata.purpose: Foundation design methods knowledge base for rigid concrete caisson (sign foundation) design
sources: Broms, B.B. (1964). Lateral Resistance of Piles in Cohesive Soils. JSMFD, ASCE, 90(SM2), 27-63
Broms, B.B. (1964). Lateral Resistance of Piles in Cohesionless Soils. JSMFD, ASCE, 90(SM3), 123-156
Brinch Hansen, J. (1961). The Ultimate Resistance of Rigid Piles Against Transversal Forces. Bulletin No. 12, Danish Geotechnical Institute
Czerniak, E. (1957). Resistance to Overturning of Single, Short Piles. ASCE J. Structural Division, 83(ST2), Paper 1188
IBC 2021, Chapter 18: Soils and Foundations, Sections 1806.2, 1807.3

broms_1964.name: Broms' Method
broms_1964.author: Bengt B. Broms
broms_1964.reference: Lateral Resistance of Piles in Cohesive/Cohesionless Soils, JSMFD ASCE Vol 90
broms_1964.description: Short rigid pile lateral capacity method. Distinguishes cohesive vs cohesionless soil, free-head vs fixed-head conditions. Widely used for single pile lateral load analysis.
broms_1964.applicability: Short rigid piles (L/D < 10-12), single soil layer, uniform profile

cohesive_soil.description: Undrained analysis using undrained shear strength (cu/Su)
assumptions: Top 1.5D of soil reaction ignored (surface wedge failure)
Ultimate soil reaction = 9 * Su * D along pile below 1.5D
Rigid pile rotates about a point near the toe

variables.Hu: Ultimate lateral load capacity (lb or kN)
variables.cu: Undrained shear strength of soil (psf or kPa)

free_head_short_pile.formula: Hu solved from moment equilibrium: Hu * (e + 1.5*D + 0.5*f) = 9*Su*D*f*(L - 1.5*D - 0.5*f)

variables.e: Load eccentricity above ground (ft or m)
variables.f: Distance below 1.5D to max moment point: f = Hu / (9 * Su * D)

free_head_short_pile.notes: Requires iterative solution or Broms charts

max_bending_moment.notes: Used to check pile structural capacity

variables.nh: Coefficient of subgrade reaction (350-700 kN/m3 typical)

cohesionless_soil.description: Drained analysis using effective friction angle and unit weight
assumptions: Soil resistance increases linearly with depth
Ultimate soil reaction = 3 * Kp * gamma * z * D
Kp = Rankine passive earth pressure coefficient

free_head_short_pile.formula: Hu = 0.5 * Kp * gamma * D * L^3 / (e + L)

variables.Hu: Ultimate lateral load capacity (lb or kN)
variables.gamma: Effective unit weight of soil (pcf or kN/m3)
variables.e: Load eccentricity above ground (ft or m)

fixed_head_short_pile.notes: Conservative simplified form for fixed-head (restrained rotation at top)

safety_factors.notes: Applied to Hu to get allowable lateral load Ha = Hu / FS

brinch_hansen_1961.name: Brinch Hansen's Method
brinch_hansen_1961.author: Jorgen Brinch Hansen
brinch_hansen_1961.reference: The Ultimate Resistance of Rigid Piles Against Transversal Forces, Bulletin No. 12, Danish Geotechnical Institute, Copenhagen
brinch_hansen_1961.description: Accounts for layered soil profiles using depth-dependent passive pressure coefficients Kq and Kc. More rigorous than Broms for complex soil conditions. Solves by moment equilibrium about rotation point.
brinch_hansen_1961.applicability: Short rigid piles, layered soils, combined c-phi soils
assumptions: Pile rotates about a single point below ground surface
Soil resistance varies with depth via Kq and Kc coefficients
Horizontal force equilibrium and moment equilibrium both satisfied

variables.pu: Ultimate soil resistance per unit length at depth z (lb/ft or kN/m)
variables.sigma_v: Effective vertical overburden pressure at depth z (psf or kPa)
variables.Kq: Passive pressure coefficient for overburden (function of phi and z/D)
variables.c: Soil cohesion at depth z (psf or kPa)
variables.Kc: Passive pressure coefficient for cohesion (function of phi and z/D)

Kq_approximation.description: Kq increases with depth ratio z/D and friction angle phi

Kc_approximation.description: Kc also increases with depth ratio z/D and friction angle phi

equilibrium_equations.horizontal_force: H = integral from 0 to x0 of pu(z)*D dz - integral from x0 to L of pu(z)*D dz
equilibrium_equations.moment_about_rotation_point: H*(e + x0) = integral from 0 to x0 of pu(z)*D*(x0-z) dz + integral from x0 to L of pu(z)*D*(z-x0) dz

variables.e: Height of load above ground (ft or m)
variables.x0: Depth to rotation point (ft or m)

equilibrium_equations.solution_method: Iterative: assume x0, solve both equations, adjust until consistent

safety_factors.notes: Lower FS acceptable due to more rigorous analysis

czerniak_1957.name: Czerniak's Method
czerniak_1957.author: Eli Czerniak
czerniak_1957.reference: Resistance to Overturning of Single, Short Piles, ASCE J. Structural Division, Vol 83, ST2, Paper 1188
czerniak_1957.description: Developed specifically for short rigid pier/caisson foundations typical of sign structures, utility poles, and refinery equipment supports. Most conservative of the common methods. Includes shaft self-weight contribution.
czerniak_1957.applicability: Short rigid piers (L/D <= 10), sign foundations, utility poles, equipment supports
assumptions: Rigid pier rotates about a point at distance 'a' below ground surface
All overturning loads resisted by passive pressure against embedment
Shaft self-weight (0.150 kcf for concrete) contributes to stability
Maximum shear at rotation point depth 'a'

rotation_point_depth.formula: a = L * [1 - sqrt(1 - (4*M_o)/(gamma_s * Kp * D * L^3))] / 2
rotation_point_depth.simplified: For uniform soil: a approximately = L/3 to L/2 depending on e/L ratio

variables.a: Depth from ground surface to rotation point (ft)
variables.M_o: Total overturning moment about ground surface (ft-lb)

overturning_moment_capacity.formula: M_resist = gamma_s * Kp * D * (a^2 * (L - a/3) - (L-a)^2 * (L - (2*L+a)/3)) / 2 + W_shaft * D/2

variables.W_shaft: Weight of concrete shaft = 0.150 * pi/4 * D^2 * L (kips)

required_embedment.description: Iterative solution: find L such that M_resist >= FS * M_applied
required_embedment.method: Trial embedment depth, compute M_resist, check >= FS * M_overturning

soil_pressure_check.check: Both must be <= allowable passive pressure at those depths

shaft_self_weight.contribution: Adds stabilizing moment = W * D/2 (acts at shaft center)

safety_factors.notes: Higher FS because method developed for critical infrastructure. Most conservative results among common methods.

ibc_1807_3.name: IBC 1807.3 Embedded Posts and Poles
ibc_1807_3.description: Prescriptive method for embedded post/pole foundations. Section 1807.3.2.1 provides the nonconstrained formula (Equation 18-1) for determining embedment depth. Limited to 12 ft max depth for lateral pressure computation.
ibc_1807_3.applicability: Posts and poles, nonconstrained (no floor slab restraint at grade), single uniform soil layer

ibc_1807_3_1_general.description: General requirements for embedded posts and poles
ibc_1807_3_1_general.requirement: Design by accepted engineering practice (PE stamp path) OR prescriptive IBC methods
ibc_1807_3_1_general.pe_stamp_note: IBC 1807.1 allows 'accepted engineering practice' path - enables Broms, Brinch Hansen, Czerniak, or FEM methods with PE certification

variables.d: Required depth of embedment (ft), max 12 ft for lateral pressure calc
variables.h: Height from ground surface to point of application of P (ft)
variables.S1: Allowable lateral soil-bearing pressure at depth = d/3, from Table 1806.2 (psf)
variables.b: Diameter of round post/footing or diagonal of square post (ft)

nonconstrained_equation_18_1.S1_calculation: S1 = table_value * (d/3), where table_value from Table 1806.2, not to exceed 15x table_value

variables.S3: Allowable lateral soil-bearing pressure at depth = 2*d/3 (psf)

constrained_equation_18_2.notes: Used when lateral constraint exists at ground surface (e.g., rigid floor slab)

limitations: No moment capacity term - cannot handle eccentric loads
Depth limited to 12 ft for lateral pressure computation
Passive resistance doubling (IBC 1806.3.4) carries risk near slopes

soil_classes.description: Sand, silty sand, clayey sand, silty gravel, clayey gravel (SW, SP, SM, SC, GM, GC)
Clay, sandy clay, silty clay, clayey silt, silt, sandy silt (CL, ML, MH, CH)

table_1806_2.depth_increase_rule: Lateral bearing may increase by tabular value per additional foot of depth, up to 15x tabular value
table_1806_2.load_combination_increase: Values may increase by 1/3 when used with ASD combinations including wind or earthquake (IBC 1605.2)
table_1806_2.sliding_restriction: For clay soils (class 5), lateral sliding resistance shall not exceed 1/2 dead load

soil_parameters.description: Typical geotechnical parameters for foundation design

soft_clay.description: Soft to firm clay, SPT N < 4

stiff_clay.description: Stiff to very stiff clay, SPT N = 8-30

skin_friction_uplift.description: Reduction factor for uplift capacity from skin friction along shaft
skin_friction_uplift.reason: Upward loading reduces confining pressure and thus friction capacity compared to compression

beta_method.description: Effective stress method for cohesionless soils

dfm_threshold.description: Drillable Foundation Method cost threshold for sign industry
dfm_threshold.cost_category_auger: Standard cost - truck-mounted auger can drill and pour in one mobilization
dfm_threshold.cost_category_excavator: Cost jump - requires excavator, shoring, possible dewatering
notes: 3 ft embedment is the practical boundary for standard auger equipment
Beyond 3 ft: cost increases 40-80% due to equipment change
Beyond 6 ft: requires engineered shoring per OSHA
Beyond 10 ft: typically requires caisson drilling rig

ibc_1807_1_reference.description: IBC 1807.1 allows foundation design by 'accepted engineering practice'
ibc_1807_1_reference.implication: PE-stamped calculations using Broms, Brinch Hansen, Czerniak, or FEM are valid alternatives to prescriptive IBC formulas
ibc_1807_1_reference.notes: This is the path Eagle Sign Co uses for engineered sign foundations - PE stamps with rational analysis methods rather than prescriptive IBC equations

method_comparison.description: Relative comparison of foundation methods for sign engineering

conservatism_ranking.notes: Least conservative - most rigorous, accounts for layered soils

recommended_for_sign_foundations.simple_ground_signs: IBC Eq 18-1 (prescriptive, no PE required if within limits)
recommended_for_sign_foundations.large_pylon_signs: Brinch Hansen or p-y analysis (PE stamped, geotechnical report required)
recommended_for_sign_foundations.all_engineered_signs: Czerniak as conservative check, Broms/Hansen for design optimization
